evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Conduct regular data audits for accuracy and consistency
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — data audits, accuracy, consistency, documentation of requirements — with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Conduct regular data audits for accuracy and consistency
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Home furnishings manufacturer's international operations analytics affecting supply chain/product decisions without financial-reporting or regulatory language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Support continuous improvement of data models and processes
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: An existing Product Data and Analytics team and established tools (Azure Data Factory, Power BI) in responsibilities indicate a growing, developing data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop and maintain Azure Data Factory pipelines
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Azure Data Factory, Power BI, SQL) with specific tasks (data audits, predictive/prescriptive analysis) provide some precision, but several bullets are generic ('Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities'), producing a mixed profile.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Assess cross-departmental requirements and build scalable solutions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Responsibilities span internal data/reporting work and cross-departmental business needs (Product Development, Supply Chain, Quality) without one clearly dominant audience.
evidence.autonomy_level: Collaborate with stakeholders to elicit, critically review, and document business requirements
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs are largely execution-oriented (collaborate, translate, develop, create, conduct, support) delivering scoped stakeholder-driven work.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing, data contracts, observability, or formal data quality framework language appears anywhere in the JD (data audits are mentioned but not framed as a testing practice).
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Conduct regular data audits for accuracy and consistency
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: regular data audits reflect an operational reliability concern, but there is no compliance or regulatory framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Support continuous improvement of data models and processes
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of developing new pipelines/solutions (greenfield) and continuous improvement/audits of existing processes (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
